MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar May-23-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Alexander Weyand for Plaintiffs Emi J. Yoshida and Alexander H. Lam.
Jonathan T. Louie for Defendants 479 Los Palmos LLC and John H. Nash (via Zoom).
John L. Kortum for Defendants Joshua Chavez and Peninsula Gold Inc. (via Zoom).

The Court heard argument on Plaintiff's motion for leave to amend the complaint. Mr. Louie raised concerns that the proposed amendment improperly attempted to name a fictitious or nonexistent entity ("Blackacre") as a defendant without sufficient factual basis or clarity, distinguishing it from standard Doe defendant substitutions. Counsel argued that the amendment lacked foundational support and created ambiguity regarding service, legal identity, and litigation participation.

Mr. Weyand responded that the proposed amendment was consistent with applicable authority and court policy favoring liberal allowance of amendments at the pleading stage.

The Court acknowledged the concerns raised by Defendants but found the objections to be premature at this stage, noting that such challenges may be more appropriately addressed through a future motion to strike or demurrer.

Following discussion and oral argument, the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED. Plaintiff to file the First Amended Complaint forthwith.

Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
